fields.helpText: Name should be in following form:   C-    for video terminals   PK-   for printers with keyboards   P-    for printers without keyboards   M-    for modems
This field is used to screan the choices that can be made at the  DEVICE TYPE question during sign-on.
This is the number of characters wide this device is.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set the top-of-form for the use of tractor-feed paper on a printer, or will clear the screen of a video display terminal.
This is the number of usable lines on the output device.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement which will cause the cursor to Back-Space.
This is the Xecutable MUMPS code which places the cursor at a XY location.
This is the Xecutable MUMPS code which places the cursor at a XY location.
This is the Xecutable MUMPS code which is used by %ZIS to OPEN the terminal.
This is the Xecutable MUMPS code which is used to CLOSE the terminal, i.e. X ^%ZIS("C").
This is the Xecutable MUMPS code which is used by %ZIS during the OPENing of the terminal to see if the device is On-Line.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a printer to 10 pitch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a printer to 12 pitch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a printer to 16 pitch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a printer to a default pitch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will use the value in the variable X to set the characters per inch display of a printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a printer to 6 lines per inch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set the printer to 8 lines per inch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set the printer to the default lines per inch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will use the value in the variable X to set the lines per inch.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on a specific letter quality mode of a printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on a specific letter quality mode of a printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on a specific letter quality mode of a printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will move the cursor to the Home position of this terminal.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn ON reverse video on a CRT.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn OFF reverse video on a CRT.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement which will erase from the cursor to the end of the line on a CRT.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement which will erase from the cursor to the end of the page on a CRT. Doing a HOME CURSOR followed by an ERASE TO END OF PAGE will erase the entire display.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement which will turn ON blinking for a CRT.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement which will turn OFF blinking for a CRT.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will RESET the device.

This is an argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn off all attributes of SELECT GRAPHIC RENDITION.   Some of the attributes of SELECT GRAPHIC RENDITION are         o  Bold or increased intensity         o  Underscore         o  Blink         o  Negative(reverse) image
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will move the print head one-half line up (for superscripts).
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will move the print head one-half line down (for subscripts).
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn ON underlineing.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn OFF underlining.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set the terminal to reverse line feed mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on  proportional spacing for a printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn ON high intensity for a CRT.  This is also used to hold the code to set a printer to BOLD or emphasize print.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn ON low intensity for a CRT. This is also used to hold the code to set a printer to REGULAR print.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will return a CRT to its NORMAL (default) intensity.  If a printer were set to BOLD, this code would reset the printer to its default setting.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate insert mode.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate replace mode.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate erasing from the beginning of the page.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate erasing the entire page.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate erasing from the beginning of a line.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate erasing the entire line.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate erasing of a single character.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate the erasing of a field given the length of the field in varariable X.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate upward movement of the cursor.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate downward movement of the cursor.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate cursor movement to the right.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate cursor movement to the left.
The value that should be entered into this field should be the number of characters produced by depressing an arrow key.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate deletion of an entire line.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate insertion of an entire line.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate deletion of a single character.
This is the argument of MUMPS WRITE statement that will indicate insertion of a single character.

This is Xecutable MUMPS code which is used whenever MailMan needs to dial a modem. The variable XMPHONE holds the number to be dialed. The variable ER is 0 if the modem dialed successfully; is 1 otherwise. The variable Y, if defined, holds a message about the dial operation, i.e. busy, no answer, etc.
This is Xecutable MUMPS code which is used whenever MailMan needs to hang up a modem.  Variable ER=0 if sucessful, =1 if not.

This is Xecutable MUMPS code which is used whenever MailMan needs to check the status of a Modem. The variable Y holds the response. This logic is used by the network diagnostics menu only, and need not be defined if the modem has no self-checking status mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on bar code printing mode of a device.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn off bar code printing mode of a device.
This field is reserved for additional bar code specifications.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on SUBSCRIPT on the printer
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn off SUBSCRIPT on the printer
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn on SUPERSCRIPT on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn off SUPERSCRIPT on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn on ITALICS on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn ITALICS on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn on COLOR on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn off COLOR on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn on GRAPHICS mode on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to turn off GRAPHICS mode on the printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the TOP LEFT CORNER part of a window in graphics mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the BOTTOM LEFT CORNER of a window in graphics mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the TOP RIGHT CORNER of a window in graphics mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the BOTTOM RIGHT CORNER of a window in graphics mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the graphics T character that joins a four box window at the middle.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the perpendicular intersection of lines at the TOP middle of a four box window.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the perpendicular intersection of lines at the BOTTOM middle of a four box window.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the perpendicular intersection of lines at the LEFT middle of a four box window.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the perpendicular intersection of lines at the RIGHT middle of a four box window.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to that produce the VERTICAL LINE in graphics mode.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to produce the HORIZONTAL LINE in graphics mode.

This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will cause the active position to move downward one line without changing column position.  If the active position is at the bottom of the screen, a scroll up is performed.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will cause the active position to move up by one.  If the cursor is at the top of the screen, a scroll down is performed.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will save the current cursor position.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will restore the cursor position.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will transfer the cursor position to the next line.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will cause characters to wrap to the next line when the right margin has been exceeded.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will cause truncation of text that exceeds the right margin limit.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will cause characters to automatically repeat when keys are depressed and held in the down position.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will disable repeat mode.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will toggle the keypad into application mode.
This the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will toggle the keypad into numeric mode.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set a value for the tab function.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will clear the settings of tabs.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will clear all tab settings.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will set the top and bottom margins of the scrolling region of a display terminal.

This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will display the top half portion of the current line with each character at double height and double wide. This argument must be used in pairs with DOUBLE HIGH DOUBLE WIDE BOTTOM on adjacent lines with the same character output.

This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will display the bottom half portion of the current line with each character at double height and double wide. This argument must be used in pairs with DOULBE HIGH DOUBLE WIDE TOP on adjacent lines with the same character output.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will display each character on the current line at a character size of double wide.
This is the argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement to restore the display of characters to the normal width on a single line.
This is a brief description of this type of terminal.
This is Xecutable MUMPS code which will set the $T switch to "TRUE" (i.e. 1) if this terminal is On-Line.
This is Xecutable MUMPS code which will OPEN the terminal's printer port.
This is Xecutable MUMPS code which will CLOSE this terminal's printer port.
The argument of a MUMPS write statement used to print the screen display onto a slaved printer.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement used to generate the same escape sequence produced by depressing the '0' key of the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '1' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '2' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '3' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '4' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '5' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '6' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '7' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '8' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '9' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'PF1' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'PF2' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'PF3' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'PF4' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '-' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the ',' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'ENTER' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the '1' key on the keypad.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'FIND' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'SELECT' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'INSERT HERE' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'REMOVE' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'PREV SCRN' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'NEXT SCRN' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'HELP' key on the keyboard.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statment used to generate the same escape sequences produced by depressing the 'DO' key on the keyboard.
Name of the default font.
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the default font.
Name of font #1
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the specified font.
Name of font #2
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the specified font.
Name of font #3
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the specified font.
Name of font #4
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the specified font.
Name of font #5
The argument of a MUMPS WRITE statement that will turn on the specified font.

The argument of a MUMPS WRITE command used to set a printer into SIMPLEX, or printing only on the first side of the page.  This field is only applicable for terminal types with a duplex printing feature such as the HP LaserJet 4 Si printers purchased by MCCR for print of encounter forms.
This should be used when printing double sided forms that will be bound at the side such as forms to be put in a binder.
This should be used when printing double sided forms that will be bound at the top, such as forms to be put in a medical record.
This is a synonym for the name of this type of terminal.
